$comment: Catalog refresh checklist for evaluating candidate Spicetify themes, extensions, and custom apps before inclusion in LibreSpot's curated catalog.
description: A measured process for adding or removing community assets. Each candidate is scored against popularity, maintenance, license, install method, Spotify-version compatibility, and Marketplace availability. The checklist produces an accept/reject/defer decision with evidence links.

evaluationCriteria.threshold: Stars > 50 preferred; < 10 stars requires exceptional utility or niche value.

signals: Response to Spicetify CLI version bumps
evaluationCriteria.threshold: Last push within 12 months. Archived repos are rejected unless no alternative exists and an explicit maintenance-risk note is added.
Must have an OSI-approved license. AGPL/GPL require download-only posture (not bundled/redistributed). No-license repos are rejected.
Prefer single-file extensions and standard theme directory layout. Multi-file or build-required assets need stronger justification and explicit install/verification steps.
Must not have open reports of breakage on the LibreSpot-pinned Spotify version. Known-broken assets are deferred until fixed upstream.

If Marketplace already provides a safer install path with version management, prefer Marketplace-only mode over bundled download. Direct inclusion is justified when Marketplace is unreliable for the asset or when the asset needs specific commit pinning.
Third-party service contact must be disclosed. Obfuscated single-file assets without readable source are rejected. External URL fetches require networkBehavior=third-party-service in the manifest.

signals: Unique functionality not covered by existing catalog
evaluationCriteria.threshold: Must provide distinct value not already covered by the existing catalog or built-in extensions.

decisionFields.lastPush: ISO 8601 date of most recent commit

evaluatedCandidates.spotifyVersionNotes: Compatible with Spotify 1.2.92; uses external lyrics API
evaluatedCandidates.reason: High demand, active maintenance, MIT license, unique synced lyrics functionality not in built-in extensions. Third-party service disclosed.

evaluatedCandidates.spotifyVersionNotes: adblock.js provides Spicetify-layer ad blocking as fallback when SpotX fails
evaluatedCandidates.reason: Provides critical SpotX fallback ad-blocking when Spotify signature protection blocks patching (SpotX #760).

evaluatedCandidates.spotifyVersionNotes: Requires theme.js injection. 4 flavors (Latte, Frappe, Macchiato, Mocha)
evaluatedCandidates.reason: Most popular community theme, very active, MIT, supports all 4 Catppuccin palettes.

evaluatedCandidates.spotifyVersionNotes: Requires theme.js. Integrates well with Spotify's sidebar layout
evaluatedCandidates.reason: Popular comfort-focused theme with active maintenance and clean design.
Unique Fluent Design aesthetic, active community, MIT license.
Unique dynamic background feature, active maintenance, popular.
Unique glassmorphism aesthetic, reasonable popularity, MIT.

rejectedExamples.reason: No license file. Cannot verify redistribution rights.
Single minified file with no readable source. Security review not possible.
Duplicates built-in Full App Display extension with fewer features.
Requires npm build step. Evaluate when LibreSpot adds build-from-source support.
